title: e2e test: Alice stdout piped but never read — deadlock risk
description: In cli/tests/e2e_payment.rs, alice_proc.stdout is set to Stdio::piped() (line 209) but is never consumed. The chat loop prints multiple lines to stdout (connecting message, MLS events, payment status lines) over the 120-second test window. On Linux, an OS pipe buffer is 64 KB. If Alice's process fills the pipe buffer, write() blocks, stalling Alice's tokio runtime — /pay never completes, and the test times out with no useful error. Fix: either set alice_proc.stdout to Stdio::inherit() (which also makes Alice's output visible in test failures, aiding diagnosis), or spawn a task that drains alice_proc.stdout.take() in a loop.
